comment_text.sn42.9:1.1: There seems to be some confusion in the location, as Nāḷandā is in Magadha, not Kosala. See notes below and another possible confusion at SN 42.11:1.1.
comment_text.sn42.9:2.1: Read duhitikā setaṭṭikā as proposed by von Hinüber (“The Ghost Word Dvı̄hitikā and the Description of Famines in Early Buddhist Literature”) and adopted by Bodhi (Connected Discourses, note on this passage).
comment_text.sn42.9:2.2: Jain tradition holds that Mahāvīra passed at a Pāvā near Nāḷandā. Buddhist texts know only the Pāvā of the Mallians to the north. Evidently there has been some confusion, and perhaps “Pāvārika’s mango grove”, the site of discussions with Jains here and at MN 56, is the Pāvā known to the Jains. Note, however, that the earliest Jain source for Mahāvīra’s passing, the Kalpasutra, does not say where Pāvā is. But it does say the events were commemorated by the rulers of Kāsī and Kosala, and the Mallians and the Licchavīs. The absence of Magadha and the presence of Malla sit better with the location of Pāvā in Malla rather than Magadha. Malla bordered Kosala and may have been considered under its sway (as was Sakya).
comment_text.sn42.9:3.6: Cf. Mahākassapa’s criticism of Ānanda at SN 16.11.
comment_text.sn42.9:3.7: Renunciates, relying on alms offered by the lay community, were careful to not put them under undue pressure. This is discussed often in the Vinaya, for example the introduction to Bu Ss 6.
comment_text.sn42.9:4.5: This is the time of the Buddha Vipassī (DN 14:1.4.1).
comment_text.sn42.9:4.6: Read saññama (“restraint”) per BJT and PTS rather than MS sāmañña (“equality”). Compare the stock list, “giving, self-control, restraint, and truthfulness” (SN 42.13:18.6).
comment_text.sn42.9:4.8: This passage is unique, but see SN 3.19:3.3, MN 14:9.3, AN 8.54:3.4. | Follow MS reading nihitaṁ vā ṭhānā vigacchati, “what is laid down disappears from its place” i.e. they lose their savings. BJT and PTS have nihitaṁ vā nādhigacchati, “does not find their savings”. Compare Kp 8:4.1, nidhi vā ṭhānā cavati, “savings shift from their place”.

root_text.sn42.9:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu cārikaṁ caramāno mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ yena nāḷandā tadavasari.
root_text.sn42.9:1.2: Tatra sudaṁ bhagavā nāḷandāyaṁ viharati pāvārikambavane.
root_text.sn42.9:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena nāḷandā dubbhikkhā hoti dvīhitikā setaṭṭhikā salākāvuttā.
root_text.sn42.9:2.2: Tena kho pana samayena nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto nāḷandāyaṁ paṭivasati mahatiyā nigaṇṭhaparisāya saddhiṁ.
root_text.sn42.9:2.3: Atha kho asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi nigaṇṭhasāvako yena nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā nigaṇṭhaṁ nāṭaputtaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho asibandhakaputtaṁ gāmaṇiṁ nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto etadavoca:
root_text.sn42.9:2.4: “ehi tvaṁ, gāmaṇi, samaṇassa gotamassa vādaṁ āropehi.
root_text.sn42.9:2.6: ‘asibandhakaputtena gāmaṇinā samaṇassa gotamassa evaṁmahiddhikassa evaṁmahānubhāvassa vādo āropito’”ti.
root_text.sn42.9:3.1: “Kathaṁ panāhaṁ, bhante, samaṇassa gotamassa evaṁmahiddhikassa evaṁmahānubhāvassa vādaṁ āropessāmī”ti?
root_text.sn42.9:3.2: “Ehi tvaṁ, gāmaṇi, yena samaṇo gotamo tenupasaṅkama; upasaṅkamitvā samaṇaṁ gotamaṁ evaṁ vadehi:
root_text.sn42.9:3.3: ‘nanu, bhante, bhagavā anekapariyāyena kulānaṁ anuddayaṁ vaṇṇeti, anurakkhaṁ vaṇṇeti, anukampaṁ vaṇṇetī’ti?
root_text.sn42.9:3.4: Sace kho, gāmaṇi, samaṇo gotamo evaṁ puṭṭho evaṁ byākaroti:
root_text.sn42.9:3.5: ‘evaṁ, gāmaṇi, tathāgato anekapariyāyena kulānaṁ anuddayaṁ vaṇṇeti, anurakkhaṁ vaṇṇeti, anukampaṁ vaṇṇetī’ti, tamenaṁ tvaṁ evaṁ vadeyyāsi:
root_text.sn42.9:3.6: ‘atha kiñcarahi, bhante, bhagavā dubbhikkhe dvīhitike setaṭṭhike salākāvutte mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ cārikaṁ carati?
root_text.sn42.9:3.7: Ucchedāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, anayāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, upaghātāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno’ti.
root_text.sn42.9:3.8: Imaṁ kho te, gāmaṇi, samaṇo gotamo ubhatokoṭikaṁ pañhaṁ puṭṭho neva sakkhati uggilituṁ, neva sakkhati ogilitun”ti.
root_text.sn42.9:3.9: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa paṭissutvā uṭṭhāyāsanā nigaṇṭhaṁ nāṭaputtaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn42.9:4.1: “Nanu, bhante, bhagavā anekapariyāyena kulānaṁ anuddayaṁ vaṇṇeti, anurakkhaṁ vaṇṇeti, anukampaṁ vaṇṇetī”ti?
root_text.sn42.9:4.2: “Evaṁ, gāmaṇi, tathāgato anekapariyāyena kulānaṁ anuddayaṁ vaṇṇeti, anurakkhaṁ vaṇṇeti, anukampaṁ vaṇṇetī”ti.
root_text.sn42.9:4.3: “Atha kiñcarahi, bhante, bhagavā dubbhikkhe dvīhitike setaṭṭhike salākāvutte mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ cārikaṁ carati?
root_text.sn42.9:4.4: Ucchedāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, anayāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, upaghātāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno”ti.
root_text.sn42.9:4.5: “Ito so, gāmaṇi, ekanavutikappe yamahaṁ anussarāmi, nābhijānāmi kiñci kulaṁ pakkabhikkhānuppadānamattena upahatapubbaṁ.
root_text.sn42.9:4.6: Atha kho yāni tāni kulāni aḍḍhāni mahaddhanāni mahābhogāni pahūtajātarūparajatāni pahūtavittūpakaraṇāni pahūtadhanadhaññāni, sabbāni tāni dānasambhūtāni ceva saccasambhūtāni ca sāmaññasambhūtāni ca.
root_text.sn42.9:4.7: Aṭṭha kho, gāmaṇi, hetū, aṭṭha paccayā kulānaṁ upaghātāya.
root_text.sn42.9:4.8: Rājato vā kulāni upaghātaṁ gacchanti, corato vā kulāni upaghātaṁ gacchanti, aggito vā kulāni upaghātaṁ gacchanti, udakato vā kulāni upaghātaṁ gacchanti, nihitaṁ vā ṭhānā vigacchati, duppayuttā vā kammantā vipajjanti, kule vā kulaṅgāroti uppajjati, yo te bhoge vikirati vidhamati viddhaṁseti, aniccatāyeva aṭṭhamīti.
root_text.sn42.9:4.9: Ime kho, gāmaṇi, aṭṭha hetū, aṭṭha paccayā kulānaṁ upaghātāya.
root_text.sn42.9:4.10: Imesu kho, gāmaṇi, aṭṭhasu hetūsu, aṭṭhasu paccayesu saṁvijjamānesu yo maṁ evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn42.9:4.11: ‘ucchedāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, anayāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, upaghātāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno’ti,
root_text.sn42.9:4.12: taṁ, gāmaṇi, vācaṁ appahāya taṁ cittaṁ appahāya taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissajjitvā yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye”ti.
root_text.sn42.9:5.1: Evaṁ vutte, asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn42.9:5.3: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhagavā dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn42.9:1.1: At one time the Buddha was wandering in the land of the Kosalans together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants when he arrived at Nāḷandā.
translation_text.sn42.9:1.2: There he stayed near Nāḷandā in Pāvārika’s mango grove.
translation_text.sn42.9:2.1: Now at that time Nāḷandā was undergoing famine and scarcity, with crops blighted and turned to straw.
translation_text.sn42.9:2.2: At that time the Jain ascetic of the Ñātika clan was residing at Nāḷandā together with a large assembly of Jain ascetics.
translation_text.sn42.9:2.3: Then Asibandhaka’s son the chief, who was a disciple of the Jains, went up to the Jain Ñātika, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Jain Ñātika said to him:
translation_text.sn42.9:2.4: “Come, chief, refute the ascetic Gotama’s doctrine.
translation_text.sn42.9:2.5: Then you will get a good reputation:
translation_text.sn42.9:2.6: ‘Asibandhaka’s son the chief refuted the doctrine of the ascetic Gotama, so mighty and powerful!’”
translation_text.sn42.9:3.1: “But sir, how am I to do this?”
translation_text.sn42.9:3.2: “Here, brahmin, go to the ascetic Gotama and say to him:
translation_text.sn42.9:3.3: ‘Sir, don’t you in many ways praise kindness, protection, and sympathy for families?’
translation_text.sn42.9:3.4: When he’s asked this, if he answers:
translation_text.sn42.9:3.5: ‘Indeed I do, chief,’ say this to him:
translation_text.sn42.9:3.6: ‘So what then are you doing, wandering together with this large Saṅgha of mendicants during a time of famine and scarcity, with crops blighted and turned to straw?
translation_text.sn42.9:3.7: The Buddha is practicing to annihilate, collapse, and ruin families!’
translation_text.sn42.9:3.8: When you put this dilemma to him, the Buddha won’t be able to either spit it out or swallow it down.”
translation_text.sn42.9:3.9: “Yes, sir,” replied Asibandhaka’s son. He got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Jain Ñātika, keeping him on his right. Then he went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn42.9:4.1: “Sir, don’t you in many ways praise kindness, protection, and sympathy for families?”
translation_text.sn42.9:4.3: “So what then are you doing, wandering together with this large Saṅgha of mendicants during a time of famine and scarcity, with crops blighted and turned to straw?
translation_text.sn42.9:4.4: The Buddha is practicing to annihilate, collapse, and ruin families!”
translation_text.sn42.9:4.5: “Well, chief, I recollect ninety-one eons back but I’m not aware of any family that’s been ruined merely by offering some cooked almsfood.
translation_text.sn42.9:4.6: Rather, rich, affluent, and wealthy families—with lots of gold and silver, lots of property and assets, and lots of money and grain—all acquired their wealth because of giving, truth, and restraint.
translation_text.sn42.9:4.7: Chief, there are eight causes and reasons for the ruin of families.
translation_text.sn42.9:4.8: Their ruin stems from rulers, bandits, fire, or flood. Or they lose their savings. Or their business fails due to not applying themselves to work. Or a wastrel is born into the family who squanders and fritters away their wealth. And impermanence is the eighth.
translation_text.sn42.9:4.9: These are the eight causes and reasons for the ruin of families.
translation_text.sn42.9:4.10: Given that these eight reasons are found, suppose someone says this:
translation_text.sn42.9:4.11: ‘The Buddha is practicing to annihilate, collapse, and ruin families!’
translation_text.sn42.9:4.12: Unless they give up that speech and that thought, and let go of that view, they will be placed in hell as if delivered there.”
translation_text.sn42.9:5.1: When he said this, Asibandhaka’s son the chief said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn42.9:5.3: From this day forth, may the Buddha remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.sn42.9:4.5: ekanavutikappe → ekanavuto kappo (bj, sya-all, km); ekanavutikappo (pts1ed)
variant_text.sn42.9:4.8: nihitaṁ vā ṭhānā vigacchati → nihitaṁ vā nādhigacchanti (bj, pts1ed) | kule vā kulaṅgāroti → kulānaṁ vā kulaṅgāro (bj); kule va kulaṅgāroti (pts1ed)
